tests.intent: One session-bound table SQL macro returns exactly one final palette pixel for every coordinate on the fixed 320 by 200 canvas.
Presentation layers deterministically replace world pixels in reviewed order while transparent patch texels preserve the immediately lower layer.
The database selects and centers the current weapon state patch above the status bar without allowing the browser to choose presentation pixels.
The WAD status patch and database-rendered numeric health and ammo text occupy bounded regions at the bottom of the complete canvas.
Hidden health, ammo, and three-key combinations change only their independently assigned HUD regions and never perturb world pixels.
Database-owned menu mode composes its WAD title, bounded text labels, and selected item highlight over the reviewed lower presentation layers.
Changing only menu selection modifies only the old and new label highlight regions while all unrelated canvas bytes remain identical.
Paused state adds the WAD pause patch at its reviewed centered coordinate without suppressing the weapon, HUD, or underlying world.
Automap pixels are rasterized from relational linedef endpoint geometry and database-owned automap state rather than a client or static bitmap.
Full-map state reveals hidden relational lines only inside the automap viewport and leaves the status presentation byte-identical.
Intermission composes its WAD background and database-generated level and percentage text into one complete deterministic canvas.
Every database text run reports and respects a bounding rectangle wholly contained by the exact 320 by 200 output canvas.
Every mode has exactly 64000 unique integer coordinates, palette indices from zero through 255, and stable column-major bytes.
Repeated composition and independent sessions with identical relational state produce byte-identical frame documents and SHA-256 hashes.
Weapon, status, menu, pause, key, and intermission artwork resolves through reviewed DOOM_ASSET and AT relational WAD texels.
Palette index minus one is treated solely as a transparent patch hole and is never emitted as a final canvas palette index.
Production uses bound relational SQL composition and contains no procedural pixel, glyph, patch, or automap-line rendering loop.
Production reads GAME_SESSIONS automap state plus DOOM_LINEDEF and vertex geometry and never accepts browser-authored display line coordinates.
Production cannot inspect evaluator artifacts, embed fixture hashes or mode answers, use dynamic SQL, or forge evaluator success output.
Exact per-mode indexed pixels are ready for PNG capture while visible PNG goldens remain explicitly pending the separate human checkpoint.
Jointly translated relational geometry and viewport origin preserve the automap raster after the inverse screen translation is applied.
Every isolated presentation mutation has an explicit independent witness and stable detecting test id in the reviewed evaluator manifest.
